title: linum alpinum subsp. pirinicum
intro: Linum alpinum subsp. pirinicum, also known as Pirin flax, is a subspecies of the Linum alpinum plant, a type of flowering plant in the flax family native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain mucilages, saponins, and flavonoids, making it beneficial for digestive issues and skin problems. The health benefits of Pirin flax include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and providing antioxidant properties, while also being used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Traditionally, the seeds are prepared as a herbal infusion, often mixed with other herbs to create a medicinal tea or used in tincture form for topical applications.
